name: Scallops Grapefruit & Fried Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the rice in the normal way and chill in the refrigerator for at least 6 hours.
2. Toss the rice with the soy sauce and set aside.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons oil in a large skillet or frying pan over high heat until almost smoking.
4. Add the onions and cook 1 minute.
5. Add corn and peppers and cook, stirring, another 2 minutes.
6. Add the snow peas and cook for 30 seconds.
7. Add rice and cook, stirring, about 5 minutes.
8. Remove from the heat and mix in the sesame oil. Arrange the rice in a ring on a serving platter. Keep warm in a 250°F oven while cooking the scallops.
9. Heat remaining tablespoon oil in a large non-reactive skillet over high heat until almost smoking.
10. Add the scallops and cook about 2 minutes without stirring, using a wooden spoon to unstick any scallops that have adhered to the bottom of the skillet.
11. Add the grapefruit juice and cook for 30 seconds. Remove the scallops with a slotted spoon and reserve on a plate.
12. Reduce heat to medium and boil the grapefruit juice until reduced by half and it starts to thicken, about 5 minutes.
13. Replace the scallops and any collected juices on the plate.
14. Cook another 30 seconds, remove from the heat, and whisk in the butter.
15. Remove the rice from the oven and pour the scallops and sauce into the center of the rice ring. Serve immediately.
